articles.headline: Stanford's Chisom Okpara to miss rest of season with injury
articles.description: Stanford basketball has announced that Chisom Okpara will miss the rest of the season due to a lower extremity injury.

articles.headline: Expected winter storm has some leagues reshuffling schedules
articles.description: An expected major winter weather system is prompting a reshuffling of games this week and threatens to wreak havoc on the weekend schedule.

articles.headline: Charles Bediako gets TRO for Alabama return in NCAA lawsuit
articles.description: Charles Bediako, a former Alabama standout who entered the NBA draft in 2023, has been granted a temporary restraining order to return to the team, a judge ruled Wednesday.

articles.headline: UNC's 'Carolina North' going forward amid hoops arena question
articles.description: North Carolina is going forward with plans to develop an off-campus expansion for academic, research, residential and mixed-use retail projects. It's too soon to say whether it will include a new arena for the university's men's basketball program.

article.headline: Cincinnati visits No. 1 Arizona following Bradley's 23-point performance
article.description: Cincinnati Bearcats (10-8, 2/3 Big 12) at Arizona Wildcats (18-0, 5-0 Big 12)
article.title: Cincinnati visits No. 1 Arizona following Bradley's 23-point performance
article.linkText: Cincinnati visits No. 1 Arizona following Bradley's 23-point performance
article.story: Cincinnati Bearcats (10-8, 2/3 Big 12) at Arizona Wildcats (18-0, 5-0 Big 12) Tucson, Arizona; Wednesday, 9 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: No. 1 Arizona hosts Cincinnati after Jaden Bradley scored 23 points in Arizona's 84-77 victory against the UCF Knights. The Wildcats have gone 10-0 at home. Arizona leads the Big 12 averaging 90.6 points and is shooting 52.0%. The Bearcats are 2/3 against Big 12 opponents. Cincinnati averages 74.1 points and has outscored opponents by 7.9 points per game. Arizona makes 52.0% of its shots from the field this season, which is 11.0 percentage points higher than Cincinnati has allowed to its opponents (41.0%). Cincinnati averages 8.6 made 3-pointers per game this season, 1.4 more made shots on average than the 7.2 per game Arizona gives up. The Wildcats and Bearcats square off Wednesday for the first time in conference play this season. TOP PERFORMERS: Koa Peat is averaging 14.7 points and 5.8 rebounds for the Wildcats. Brayden Burries is averaging 16.7 points over the last 10 games. Kerr Kriisa is averaging 7.3 points and 3.8 assists for the Bearcats. Baba is averaging 12.6 points over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Wildcats: 10-0, averaging 92.3 points, 42.0 rebounds, 18.0 assists, 8.6 steals and 4.4 blocks per game while shooting 51.2% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 71.0 points per game. Bearcats: 4-6, averaging 72.9 points, 34.8 rebounds, 17.3 assists, 6.2 steals and 4.1 blocks per game while shooting 42.4% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 68.4 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar.
